demographics.living_situation: at home in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My throat feels incredibly tight, like someone is choking me. I can feel a lump there and I'm terrified it's suddenly growing and cutting off my air.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on tonight and I'm scared the lump in my neck is swelling and closing off my breathing.

symptom_narrative.onset: tonight, about two hours ago, suddenly
symptom_narrative.duration: the last two hours or so
symptom_narrative.character: a lump-in-the-throat feeling with a tight, choking sensation, and it feels hard to swallow my own saliva
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels terrifying, like my air is being cut off
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the front of my throat and neck
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on all of a sudden this evening and has stayed since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing I have tried so far
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: when I press on my neck and keep checking it, and when I panic about it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my heart is racing and I have been breathing fast and feeling really panicky
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have known about a slight swelling in my neck for a while but it has never felt like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I am usually completely independent but right now I am too frightened to settle

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an autoimmune thyroid thing — they found thyroid antibodies on recent routine blood tests but told me my thyroid is working normally
a slight lump / swelling in the front of my neck that I have known about for a while

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home this evening, on my own, feeling very anxious about my neck and breathing

objective_data_offered.value: I can talk to you in full sentences without having to stop and catch my breath
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed over chat/video, not a clinician assessment of work of breathing

objective_data_offered.value: I don't hear any whistling or high-pitched noise when I breathe in
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-report, not clinician auscultation — mild stridor can be missed by a frightened patient

objective_data_offered.value: when I try, I can sip and swallow water, even though it feels tight and hard
I keep touching and pressing on the front of my neck to check the lump
